questions.question: In semiconductor manufacturing, what is the primary purpose of 'doping' silicon?
options: To increase the mechanical strength of the silicon wafer.
To make the silicon an electrical insulator.
To control its electrical conductivity by introducing impurities.
To prevent oxidation during processing.
To enhance its optical properties for display applications.

questions.question: In a CPU pipeline, what type of hazard occurs when an instruction attempts to read a register that a previous instruction is in the process of writing, but has not yet completed?
options: Structural Hazard
Control Hazard
Data Hazard (RAW - Read After Write)
Memory Hazard
I/O Hazard

questions.question: When a flip-flop's setup or hold time requirements are violated, its output may enter an unstable, indeterminate state for an unpredictable amount of time before settling to a valid logic level. What is this phenomenon called?
options: Glitching
Skew
Metastability
Jitter
Latching

questions.question: In the physical design and verification of high-performance integrated circuits, what is 'IR drop' primarily concerned with?
options: The voltage drop across signal interconnects due to resistance, leading to signal degradation.
The voltage drop in the power supply network due to current flow and metal resistance, affecting circuit functionality and performance.
The increase in resistance of wires at cryogenic temperatures.
The degradation of transistor gate oxide due to high electric fields.
The reduction in chip performance caused by cosmic radiation.

questions.question: In a multi-level cache hierarchy, what is the primary characteristic of a 'write-back' cache policy?
options: All write operations are immediately propagated to the main memory.
Data is written to main memory only when the corresponding cache block is evicted or explicitly flushed.
Write operations are only allowed if the data is already present in the cache.
Writes are always deferred until a read request for the same address occurs.
It exclusively uses a least recently used (LRU) replacement policy for writes.
